text: Inclusion education policies aim to achieve social equality, but often cause unintended, even opposite effects. This phenomenon seems to occur more frequently when equality of access is subordinated to politically charged notions of social integration. Within this context, this chapter aims to contribute to the urgent need to rethink the current inclusion strategies used in Catalonia’s compulsory education schools and Vocational Training Institutes (VTPs). This chapter undertakes a thematic literature and policy review of both past and present experiences and interventions. Furthermore, it examines the inherent problems and obstacles in the education system that hinder the realisation of the policy aims, specifically in relation to the distribution of tasks and competences in relation to educational programs for the inclusion of disadvantaged students.
